title: Tune query parameters for the recall-latency tradeoff
description: Raise ef_search (HNSW) or nprobe (IVFFlat) until recall meets target, then stop, since both cost latency.
body: Use when an ANN search returns too few correct neighbours or runs too slow. Do not leave the query-time search parameter at its default and assume recall is adequate; ef_search for HNSW and nprobe for IVFFlat directly trade recall for latency. Actions: build a recall@k benchmark against brute-force ground truth, sweep the parameter upward, and choose the smallest value meeting the recall target. Verify p99 latency at that setting fits the budget. Validation signals include recall@k crossing the threshold and latency staying within budget at the chosen value. Risk boundary: pushing the parameter very high to chase the last percent of recall can blow the latency budget, so set an explicit recall target and stop there rather than maximising blindly.
